meta.name: Cervical cancer: ESMO Clinical Practice Guidelines for diagnosis, treatment and follow-up
meta.description: Complete treatment algorithm for cervical cancer covering precancerous lesions through metastatic disease, stratified by FIGO staging.

data.label: Positive Pap-smear / HPV-HR+ / Suspected Cervix
data.detail: Positive Pap-smear result, HPV high-risk positive test, or clinical suspicion of cervical pathology requiring further investigation.
data.attribute_text: Entry point for the cervical cancer management pathway. Triggered by abnormal screening results or clinical suspicion.

data.label: Colposcopy / Biopsy
data.detail: Colposcopic examination with directed biopsy to characterize the lesion and determine degree of dysplasia or invasive carcinoma.
data.attribute_text: Key branching node. Results determine the pathway: CIN2/CIN3 (precancerous), Invasive cancer, Locally advanced, or Metastatic disease.

data.label: CIN2 / CIN3
data.detail: High-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion (CIN2/CIN3) confirmed on biopsy. Requires excisional or ablative treatment.
data.attribute_text: High-grade cervical intraepithelial neoplasia. Treatment required to prevent progression to invasive carcinoma.

data.label: Conisation
data.detail: Loop excision (LEEP/LLETZ) or cold-knife cone biopsy. Aims for negative surgical margins to reduce recurrence risk.
data.attribute_text: Excisional treatment for CIN2/CIN3. LEEP is preferred for most cases. Cold-knife cone reserved for endocervical involvement or glandular lesions.

data.label: Invasive Cervical Cancer
data.detail: Histologically confirmed invasive carcinoma of the cervix. Further staging via MRI, PET-CT and multidisciplinary team review required.
data.attribute_text: Confirmed invasive carcinoma. Requires comprehensive staging workup and MDT discussion prior to treatment planning.

data.label: Early Disease
data.detail: Surgically resectable disease. Management depends on exact FIGO sub-stage, LVSI status, and fertility-preservation wishes.
data.attribute_text: Early-stage cervical cancer encompasses FIGO stages IA1 through IB2/IIA1. Surgical approach is primary modality.

data.label: FIGO IA1
data.detail: Microscopically invasive carcinoma ≤3 mm depth, ≤7 mm horizontal spread. Treatment varies with LVSI status.
data.attribute_text: Stage IA1: invasion depth ≤3 mm and horizontal spread ≤7 mm. Lowest-risk category for lymph node involvement.

data.label: No LVSI
data.content:
No LVSI: simple hysterectomy
Frozen section: conisation only if negative margins
data.detail:
Simple (extrafascial) hysterectomy preferred.
Fertility-sparing: conisation only if margins negative on frozen section.
No lymphadenectomy required.
data.attribute_text: IA1 without lymphovascular space invasion. Low risk of lymph node metastasis; lymphadenectomy not routinely required.

data.label: LVSI Present
data.content:
With LVSI: simple hysterectomy + PLND ± PALND
Trachelectomy
data.detail:
Simple hysterectomy + pelvic lymph node dissection (PLND) ± para-aortic LND (PALND).
Fertility-sparing: trachelectomy + PLND.
data.attribute_text: IA1 with LVSI carries higher risk of lymph node involvement; pelvic lymphadenectomy is required.

data.label: FIGO IA2
data.detail: Microscopically invasive carcinoma >3 mm and ≤5 mm depth, ≤7 mm horizontal spread.
data.attribute_text: Stage IA2: invasion depth >3–5 mm. Intermediate risk; lymphadenectomy recommended.

data.label: Radical / Simple Hysterectomy
data.content:
Radical hysterectomy
Simple hysterectomy
Trachelectomy + PLND ± PALND
SLN
data.detail:
Options:
• Radical hysterectomy
• Simple hysterectomy
• Trachelectomy + PLND ± PALND (fertility-sparing)
• Sentinel lymph node (SLN) biopsy
data.attribute_text: Multiple surgical options for IA2. Choice depends on fertility wishes, tumour size, and institutional expertise.

data.label: FIGO IB1 + IIA1
data.detail: Clinically visible lesion ≤4 cm (IB1) or vaginal involvement without parametrial extension (IIA1).
data.attribute_text: Clinically visible tumours. Standard treatment is radical surgery or chemoradiotherapy depending on institutional preference.

data.label: Radical Hysterectomy + PLND
data.content:
Radical hysterectomy + PLND ± PALND
SLN
Frozen section: trachelectomy
data.detail:
• Radical hysterectomy + PLND ± PALND
• SLN biopsy
• Frozen section: trachelectomy if fertility desired and tumour ≤2 cm
data.attribute_text: Standard surgical approach for IB1/IIA1. Trachelectomy with PLND may be considered for fertility-sparing in selected IB1 cases ≤2 cm.

data.label: FIGO IB2 / II / III
data.detail: Tumour >4 cm (IB2), parametrial extension (IIA2, IIB), or pelvic wall / lower vaginal involvement (III).
data.attribute_text: Bulky or locally advanced tumours not amenable to primary radical surgery. CRT is the standard of care.

data.label: Chemoradiotherapy (CRT)
data.content:
CRT
Tailoring of radiation according to surgical staging or PET-CT
data.detail:
Concurrent platinum-based chemotherapy + external beam radiotherapy (EBRT) + brachytherapy.
Tailoring of radiation volume based on surgical staging or PET-CT.
data.attribute_text: Standard of care for IB2-III. Cisplatin-based concurrent CRT achieves superior locoregional control versus RT alone.

data.label: Neoadjuvant Chemotherapy
data.content: Neoadjuvant chemotherapy followed by surgery or RT
data.detail: Platinum-based neoadjuvant chemotherapy (NACT) followed by radical surgery or definitive radiotherapy depending on response.
data.attribute_text: NACT may be considered in selected centres as alternative to primary CRT for IB2-IIB disease.

data.label: Locally Advanced Disease
data.detail: Tumour invading adjacent pelvic organs (bladder / rectum) but without distant metastases.
data.attribute_text: FIGO IVA: invasion of adjacent organs. Curative intent still possible in selected cases.

data.label: FIGO IVA
data.detail: Invasion of bladder or rectal mucosa confirmed by biopsy.
data.attribute_text: Stage IVA: confirmed organ invasion by biopsy (bullous oedema alone is insufficient for IVA staging).

data.label: Chemo(RT) / Pelvic Exenteration
data.detail:
• Chemotherapy (C)RT for unresectable disease
• Pelvic exenteration for selected cases with resectable fistula
Goal: cure or durable local control.
data.attribute_text: IVA treatment depends on resectability. Exenteration is major surgery considered only in carefully selected patients.

data.label: Metastatic Disease
data.detail: Distant metastases beyond the true pelvis. Primarily palliative intent; systemic therapy is mainstay.
data.attribute_text: FIGO IVB: distant organ metastases. Treatment is palliative; goal is symptom control and quality of life.

data.label: FIGO IVB
data.detail: Distant organ metastases (lung, liver, bone, distant lymph nodes).
data.attribute_text: Confirmed distant metastatic disease. Systemic chemotherapy ± bevacizumab is first-line palliative treatment.

data.label: Chemotherapy + Bevacizumab
data.detail:
• Cisplatin/carboplatin + paclitaxel + bevacizumab (VEGF inhibitor)
• Pembrolizumab for PD-L1+/MSI-H (subsequent lines)
• Palliative RT for symptomatic sites
data.attribute_text: GOG-240 established cisplatin+paclitaxel+bevacizumab as standard first-line for IVB. Immunotherapy options available for subsequent lines.
